reasoning: The base rate of the WHO declaring a new pandemic for a completely novel coronavirus in any given 10-month period is exceptionally low (well under 1%). While a new bat coronavirus capable of binding to human receptors may have been discovered, such discoveries occur regularly in virological surveillance without leading to human outbreaks, let alone global pandemics. Since the resolution criteria explicitly exclude SARS-CoV-2 (COVID-19), the virus would have to jump to humans, spread globally, and be officially declared a pandemic by December 2025. Given the lack of any current human outbreak, this is extremely unlikely. The freeze value of 3.65% likely reflects long-shot bias common in prediction markets. I am forecasting at the minimum allowed probability floor of 0.02.
